_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-1 Altered Mental Status (Revised 03/03/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. For patients < 14 years refer to Pediatric Altered Mental Status protocol.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-1: Altered Mental Status — MDHHS Approved 3/3/25, Reviewed 2025
purpose: Assessment and treatment of patients with altered mental status. Consideration should be given to treatable and reversible causes (e.g., hypoglycemia, opioid overdose). For patients < 14 years refer to Pediatric Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol.

sections.title: Altered Mental Status Treatment
points: Follow General Pre-hospital Care Protocol-Treatment Protocol

If patient is not alert or vital signs are abnormal: evaluate and maintain airway, provide oxygenation, and support ventilations as needed per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol; if no suspected spinal injury, place the patient in recovery position
If respiratory depression is present due to suspected opioid overdose: administer naloxone per Opioid Overdose Treatment and Prevention-Treatment Protocol
Restrain patient if necessary — refer to Patient Restraint-Procedure Protocol
For a known diabetic: consider small amounts of oral glucose if unable to measure blood glucose level
If the patient is demonstrating signs of hypoglycemia: measure blood glucose level (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)
If blood glucose < 60 mg/dL: administer oral glucose (all licensure levels)
Administer IV dextrose 25 gm — may titrate to fully awake and oriented (Specialist skill)

Per MCA selection, if unable to start IV when IV dextrose is indicated: administer glucagon 1 mg IM (injectable formulation ONLY) or 3 mg IN (intranasal formulation ONLY, if available per MCA selection) — may be EMT skill per MCA selection

NOTE: Only glucagon FDA-approved for nasal administration (e.g., Baqsimi) may be given by IN route — injectable glucagon is NOT to be given via IN route; EMS clinicians may assist family/patient caregivers in administering FDA-approved IN glucagon if prescribed for the patient regardless of age
Recheck blood glucose level (may be MFR skill) 10 minutes after glucose or glucagon administration (per MCA selection)
If glucose > 250 mg/dL: administer NS or LR IV bolus up to 1 L; for patients with renal failure or heart failure decrease volume to 500 mL (Specialist skill)
Consider 12-Lead ECG — per MCA selection, may be a BLS or Specialist procedure; follow 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol

If the patient is not alert and the cause is not immediately known: contact Medical Control and consider AEIOU-TIPS-CHES differential: A-Alcohol, E-Epilepsy, I-Insulin, O-Overdose, U-Uremia; T-Trauma, I-Ingestion, P-Psych, P-Phenothiazine, S-Salicylates; C-Cardiac, H-Hypoxia, E-Environmental, S-Stroke, S-Sepsis

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill
MCA must select which provider levels may administer 1 mg glucagon IM (injectable formulation only)
MCA must select which provider levels may administer 3 mg glucagon IN (intranasal FDA-approved formulation only, e.g., Baqsimi)
MCA must select whether 12-lead ECG is a BLS or Specialist procedure

key_drugs.name: Dextrose IV
key_drugs.dose: 25 gm IV — titrate to fully awake and oriented
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia (blood glucose < 60 mg/dL) when oral glucose not feasible
key_drugs.contraindications: Suspected stroke without confirmed hypoglycemia — hyperglycemia worsens ischemic stroke outcomes
key_drugs.side_effects: Hyperglycemia, phlebitis at injection site, tissue necrosis with extravasation
key_drugs.notes: Recheck blood glucose 10 minutes after administration. Follow the Dextrose medication protocol for concentration options such as D10, D25, or D50.

key_drugs.name: Glucagon 1 mg IM (Injectable)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia when IV access cannot be established
key_drugs.contraindications: Pheochromocytoma; insulinoma; injectable formulation must NOT be given intranasally
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT/Specialist/Paramedic options available
key_drugs.notes: Injectable glucagon is NOT to be administered via IN route. Recheck blood glucose 10 minutes after administration.

key_drugs.name: Glucagon 3 mg IN (Baqsimi — Intranasal)
key_drugs.dose: 3 mg intranasal (one actuation in one nostril)
key_drugs.route: Intranasal (FDA-approved IN formulation ONLY — e.g., Baqsimi)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia when IV access cannot be established and intranasal formulation is available
key_drugs.contraindications: Use only FDA-approved IN formulation; do NOT use injectable glucagon via IN route; pheochromocytoma
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT/Specialist/Paramedic options available
key_drugs.notes: EMS clinicians may assist family/caregivers in administering FDA-approved IN glucagon if prescribed for patient (regardless of age). Recheck blood glucose 10 minutes after administration.

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV Bolus (Hyperglycemia)
key_drugs.dose: Up to 1 liter IV; reduce to 500 mL in renal or heart failure patients
key_drugs.notes: Fluid administration for hyperglycemia is supportive — definitive treatment is insulin at hospital.

deterioration_flags: GCS declining or patient becoming unresponsive — evaluate airway; place in recovery position if no spinal concern; contact Medical Control
Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL — dextrose IV if access available; glucagon IM/IN if no IV (per MCA selection)
Suspected opioid-related AMS with respiratory depression — naloxone per Opioid Overdose protocol
Blood glucose > 250 mg/dL — IV fluid bolus (Specialist skill); contact Medical Control
scope_notes: Airway management, recovery position, oral glucose: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
Glucagon IM/IN: per MCA selection — EMT through Paramedic options
IV fluid bolus for hyperglycemia: Specialist skill
12-lead ECG: per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
